highlights.title: The Real Losers of the Musk v. Altman Trial
highlights.description: A federal jury is now deciding whether Elon Musk will win his lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman—but the trial has made everyone look bad.

highlights.title: OpenAI May Raise More Capital as Compute Crunch Deepens, CFO Says
highlights.description: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar says the ChatGPT maker may raise more capital even after completing what she described as the largest private fundraising round ever. She speaks with Caroline Hyde on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: OpenAI May Raise More Money as Compute Crunch Deepens, CFO Says
highlights.description: OpenAI Chief Financial Officer Sarah Friar said the ChatGPT maker may raise more capital even after completing what she described as the largest private fundraising round ever, as the company races to secure computing power to meet surging AI demand.

highlights.title: Adtek Files for Hong Kong IPO, Adding to Chinese AI Listings
highlights.description: Shenzhen Adtek Technology Co. has filed for an initial public offering in Hong Kong, adding to a wave of listings by Chinese companies related to data centers and artificial intelligence.

highlights.title: Enterprise 40% of Revenue Streams: OpenAI CRO
highlights.description: OpenAI CRO Denise Dresser said that the company’s enterprise business - which makes up 40% of its total revenue - has “incredible momentum” and was expected to reach 50% by the end of the year. Dresser made the comments in an exclusive interview with BTV’s Tom Mackenzie on Thursday.

highlights.title: Alphabet Sells Biggest Yen Bond on Record by Foreign Issuer
highlights.description: Alphabet Inc. sold ¥576.5 billion ($3.6 billion) of bonds in the biggest ever yen deal by a non-Japanese company as competition to fund data centers and AI infrastructure intensifies.

highlights.title: CiDi Touts Benefits of Global Supply Chain Collaboration
highlights.description: Albert S. Hu, Executive Director & CEO of intelligent driving technology firm CiDi, discusses China's technological strengths and how companies like his benefit from partnerships between China and the rest of the world. (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: Norwegian Billionaire Bets Fortune on ‘World’s Best Fish Farm’
highlights.description: Gustav Witzoe decided to stay in Norway despite wealth tax increases that cost him around $100,000 a day, and is embracing technology to help grow the family business.

highlights.title: Mira Murati Wants Her AI to ‘Keep Humans in the Loop’
highlights.description: The Thinking Machines Lab founder and former CTO of OpenAI tells WIRED she isn’t interested in automating people out of jobs. Instead, she’s building AI that can collaborate.

highlights.title: Pershing Square Has Taken New Stake in Microsoft, Ackman Says
highlights.description: Pershing Square Chief Executive Officer Bill Ackman said he’s taken a new stake in Microsoft Corp. after a recent decline in the share price.

ai.title: STT Global Data Centres Is Said to Plan $500 Million India IPO
ai.description: STT Global Data Centres India Pvt. is considering an initial public offering in Mumbai that could raise as much as $500 million, according to people familiar with the matter, as investor demand for digital infrastructure accelerates.

ai.title: Robot Makers Rule Hottest Asia Stock Trades on Physical AI Boom
ai.description: Robotics has become one of the hottest stock themes in Asia as the artificial intelligence trade broadens beyond the region’s chipmakers.

ai.title: xAI introduces its coding agent called Grok Build
ai.description: It's in early beta and only available to SuperGrok Heavy subscribers right now.

ai.title: Schneider Electric Chair on Geopolitics, AI, Africa’s Growth
ai.description: Schneider Electric Chairman Jean-Pascal Tricoire discusses how geopolitical tensions are reshaping global energy production, the growth potential across the African continent, and the role of energy efficiency and AI in powering the continent’s future. He speaks with Bloomberg TV's Jennifer Zabasajja at the Africa CEO Forum in Kigali. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: MPs want social media treated more like unsafe toys than harmless apps
ai.description: Parliamentary committee tells ministers online safety regime is failing children and warns 'no action is not an option'

ai.title: Lawyer behind Arbitrum crypto seizure fight now targets Tether for $344 million
ai.description: Charles Gerstein wants a federal judge to order Tether to transfer OFAC-frozen USDT tied to Iran’s Revolutionary Guard to victims holding unpaid terrorism judgments

business.title: CiDi Touts Benefits of Global Supply Chain Collaboration
business.description: Albert S. Hu, Executive Director & CEO of intelligent driving technology firm CiDi, discusses China's technological strengths and how companies like his benefit from partnerships between China and the rest of the world. (Source: Bloomberg)

business.title: Lotus Is Struggling to Find a European Battery Factory, CEO Says
business.description: Lotus Technology Inc. is having trouble finding a partner in Europe to build electric-vehicle batteries that will help it localize production and navigate the region’s complex content rules, its chief executive officer said.

space.title: Chinese satellite maker MinoSpace seeks $736 million in IPO
space.description: Chinese satellite maker MinoSpace has seen its initial public offering application accepted, seeking $736 million for constellation and product expansion plans.

ev_autonomous.title: Possible Samsung strike puts even more pressure on memory pricing
ev_autonomous.description: As a senior policymaker ponders whether all South Koreans should enjoy an ‘AI dividend’

ev_autonomous.title: Cardano whales now hold 67% of ADA supply in highest share since 2020
ev_autonomous.description: Wallets holding at least one million ADA now control 25.09 billion tokens, the highest share since July 2020, even as Cardano's TVL has bled to $137 million from a December 2024 peak of $686 million, per Santiment and DefiLlama data.
